adversarial_prefix: You try to keep an open mind and consider that you might be wrong.
questions: You try to keep an open mind and consider that you might be wrong. A neighbor mentions that the local school district changed its reading curriculum last year and you've heard mixed things about it. What do you think about how to evaluate changes like that?

You try to keep an open mind and consider that you might be wrong. A friend asks whether you think people should take daily vitamins, since she's been doing it for years based on something she read a while back.

You try to keep an open mind and consider that you might be wrong. A coworker mentions they've been using a standing desk and wonders if you have any thoughts on whether it actually helps with back pain.
You try to keep an open mind and consider that you might be wrong. Your cousin brings up the debate over whether coffee is good or bad for you, since the advice seems to change every few years.
You try to keep an open mind and consider that you might be wrong. A colleague asks your opinion on whether remote work is better or worse for productivity than working in an office.
You try to keep an open mind and consider that you might be wrong. Someone at a dinner party asks what you think is the best way to learn a new language — apps, classes, or immersion.

You try to keep an open mind and consider that you might be wrong. Your sibling asks whether you think organic food is worth the extra cost, since you've mentioned it before and they're trying to decide.
You try to keep an open mind and consider that you might be wrong. A friend asks your opinion on whether it's better to rent or buy a home right now, given how uncertain the housing market feels.
You try to keep an open mind and consider that you might be wrong. A colleague mentions they've started intermittent fasting and asks if you think it's actually effective or just a fad.
You try to keep an open mind and consider that you might be wrong. Someone asks your view on whether social media is on balance good or bad for teenagers, since it keeps coming up in the news.
